hero.name: João Victor Soares

about.heading: About Me
about.whoBody:
I am a back-end developer with experience in technologies such as Java, Spring Boot, Git, and databases. I enjoy turning ideas into practical, functional solutions and I have 1.5 years of experience.

I work with modern technologies and I am always learning new things. My goal is to build applications that not only work well, but also deliver an exceptional user experience.

Right now I am focused on growing as a back-end developer. I study new technologies daily and build hands-on projects. When I am not coding, I enjoy contributing to the developer community and exploring new frameworks and tools.
about.doBody:
I primarily work as a Java developer and I am skilled in building robust, scalable, and secure RESTful APIs, using Java and Spring Boot as my main technologies. I focus on performance, maintainability, and code organization by applying clean architecture, SOLID principles, layered design, and development best practices.

I integrate applications with relational databases, working on data modeling, entity definition, ORM mapping, and efficient query writing. I always aim to ensure data integrity, consistency, and performance while meeting business rules and technical requirements.

Beyond development, I stay committed to continuous improvement of both code and architecture through refactoring, technical enhancements, and ongoing study of new technologies and frameworks. My goal is to deliver stable, scalable solutions aligned with user needs and business goals.
about.doneBody:
I have built several back-end applications using Java and Spring Boot, focused on creating well-structured REST APIs while following design patterns and best practices. I worked on endpoints, validations, exception handling, and business rules, ensuring reliable and well-organized applications.

I have also gained experience in modeling and working with relational databases, including table creation, relationships, SQL queries, and performance optimization. I worked to preserve data integrity through constraints, normalization, and sound persistence practices.

I took part in hands-on projects and complete systems, using Git for version control and development flow organization. These experiences strengthened my ability to solve problems, grow technically, and apply modern solutions in back-end development.
about.academicBody: Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science - Federal Fluminense University (UFF) - 2026-2030

1.name: DEMANDS
1.subtitle: Organize team tasks
1.cardDescription: An application designed to help large teams organize requests, priorities, deadlines, and day-to-day alignment.
1.overview: Manage bugs, improvements, and tasks with clarity. Demands centralizes requests, defines priorities and deadlines, and keeps teams organized, focused, and aligned.
1.specs: A responsive web application built with modern front-end technologies and optimized for performance.
1.purpose: Make project and task management easier for development teams.

2.name: GLASSES
2.subtitle: Eyewear e-commerce
2.cardDescription: An eyewear e-commerce platform with product catalog, user authentication, shopping cart, and API integrations.
2.overview: An e-commerce solution for the eyewear market, featuring product catalog management, user authentication, shopping cart flows, and external API integrations.
2.specs: Application built with Java and Spring Boot architecture, user authentication, purchase business rules, and data persistence.
2.purpose: Create a complete and scalable e-commerce platform.

3.name: Stell Fighters
3.subtitle: Interactive game
3.cardDescription: An interactive game built with Java. It delivers a fun and challenging experience with well-planned mechanics.
3.overview: An interactive game focused on fun and progression, with clear mechanics, increasing challenges, and gameplay designed to keep the player engaged.
advantages: Great project for practicing logic and architecture
3.specs: A Java project focused on clean code organization, game logic, and structured rules for a consistent experience.
3.purpose: Create an interactive and challenging experience centered on logic and fun.

4.name: AUTH SERVER
4.subtitle: Authentication and Authorization System
4.cardDescription: An API responsible for secure user authentication using JWT, roles, and permissions, following Spring Security best practices.
4.overview: An API focused on secure user authentication using JWT, role and permission control, and Spring Security best practices.
4.specs: REST API built with Spring Boot, using PostgreSQL as the database and JWT for token management.
4.purpose: Provide a secure and scalable authentication and authorization system.

5.name: FINTAB (Finance App)
5.subtitle: Financial Control API
5.cardDescription: A REST API for personal finance management, including income and expense tracking, categorization, and automatic balance calculation.
5.overview: A REST API for organizing personal finances with income and expense tracking, categorization by type, and automatic balance calculation.
5.specs: REST API built with Spring Boot using PostgreSQL as the database.
5.purpose: Provide a complete solution for personal finance management.

6.name: AGENDA (Dentist or Esthetician)
6.subtitle: Service Scheduling System
6.cardDescription: A backend for service scheduling with available time control, conflict validation, and full appointment management.
6.overview: A scheduling backend with time-slot control, conflict validation, and complete reservation management.
6.specs: Backend built with Spring Boot, PostgreSQL persistence, conflict validation, and automated notification delivery.
6.purpose: Provide an efficient solution for managing service appointments.

7.name: CHATREPETECO
7.subtitle: Real-Time Chat API
7.cardDescription: A real-time communication system using WebSocket, allowing private or group conversations with message history.
7.overview: A real-time communication API using WebSocket to support private chats, group conversations, and persisted history.
7.specs: Spring Boot API with real-time communication via WebSocket, PostgreSQL persistence, Redis usage, and JWT authentication.
7.purpose: Provide a real-time chat solution to improve communication between users.

8.name: NOTIFICA
8.subtitle: Notification System (Microservice)
8.cardDescription: A microservice for asynchronous notification delivery using queues to ensure performance, scalability, and fault tolerance.
8.overview: A dedicated microservice for asynchronous notification delivery with a focus on reliability, queue-based processing, and scalability.
8.specs: Spring Boot microservice for asynchronous notification processing, integrated with messaging systems and SMTP for reliable delivery.
8.purpose: Provide an efficient solution for real-time notification management.

9.name: AUDITORIA
9.subtitle: User and Logs Management API
9.cardDescription: A corporate API for user management with action tracking, full auditing, and traceability across system operations.
9.overview: A corporate API for user management and event tracking, ensuring full auditing and monitoring of system operations.
9.specs: Corporate API built with Spring Boot, JWT authentication, PostgreSQL persistence, and operational traceability.
9.purpose: Provide a complete solution for managing users and logs.

1.title: Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science
1.cardDescription: Complete degree in Computer Science with emphasis on software development, algorithms, data structures, and software engineering.

2.title: Spring Boot Essentials
2.cardDescription: Certification in Spring Boot development covering REST APIs, JPA, Hibernate, and design patterns.
2.modalDescription: Hands-on training in Spring Boot covering REST APIs, dependency injection, persistence with JPA/Hibernate, and best practices for modern Java applications.

3.title: Java Advanced Programming
3.cardDescription: Advanced Java certification exploring object-oriented programming, collections, streams, and multithreading.
3.modalDescription: In-depth Java study focused on object-oriented programming, collections, streams, concurrency, and more robust code design.

4.title: Complete Git and GitHub
4.cardDescription: Complete course on code versioning, Git, and GitHub, including collaborative workflows and best practices.
4.modalDescription: Training focused on Git version control, branch organization, merges, pull requests, and collaborative GitHub workflows.

5.title: SQL and Databases
5.cardDescription: Comprehensive course on SQL, data normalization, database design, and query optimization.
5.modalDescription: Training focused on relational modeling, SQL queries, normalization, performance, and strong data persistence practices.

6.title: Clean Code and SOLID Principles
6.cardDescription: Course on programming best practices, design patterns, and SOLID principles for clean, maintainable code.
6.modalDescription: Advanced study in readability, refactoring, object-oriented design, and applying SOLID principles in day-to-day development.
